config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan di Indonesia, keputusan atas nama “kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan bagi orang kecil. Masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak pribadi dihormati, lalu kepentingan bersama dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter a lot, but they should not automatically outrank individual rights. From my perspective as someone from China, I have seen how appeals to the collective can be used to achieve real public goods like order, infrastructure, and poverty reduction, but they can also become a convenient excuse to silence ordinary people or ignore fairness. If individual rights are treated as secondary by default, then it becomes too easy for authorities or majorities to decide what is best for everyone without real accountability. A healthy society needs a balance: strong public interests, but also clear limits that protect the individual from being sacrificed too easily.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara otomatis atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, dari urusan lahan sampai kebebasan berpendapat, jadi saya tidak percaya kalau hak individu bisa diposisikan sekunder begitu saja. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dicapai lewat batas yang jelas agar negara atau mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak orang sambil tetap mengejar kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I would not give society automatic priority over the individual, because in my view that is too easy to abuse. From my perspective as someone from China, collective goals can produce real benefits such as stability and development, but I have also seen how the language of the common good can be used to override fairness and silence ordinary people. If individual rights are treated as secondary by default, then the burden always falls on the weaker person to prove they deserve protection, which is not a healthy standard. The better principle is that society’s interests matter, but they must be pursued within firm limits that protect the individual from being sacrificed too readily.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diutamakan di atas hak individu. Bagi saya sebagai orang Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, jadi kalau hak individu ditempatkan sebagai nomor dua, itu membuka pintu penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibatasi oleh hak yang jelas supaya mayoritas atau negara tidak seenaknya menentukan siapa yang boleh dikorbankan. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu mengejar kebaikan bersama tanpa menginjak martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically outrank the individual, even though I come from a culture that values collective goals and social stability. From my perspective in China, I have seen how appeals to the common good can genuinely help with development and public order, but I have also seen how easily that language can be used to pressure ordinary people and excuse unfair treatment. If the individual is always placed second, then the burden of proof falls on the weakest person, which feels unjust and dangerous to me. Society’s interests matter, but they should be pursued with firm limits that protect basic rights, not by default sacrificing the person for the group.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
